title: Perioral dermatitis
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact differential section supports documenting a perioral eruption context, onset and persistence, papular or pustular morphology, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning or soreness, distribution, and relevant corticosteroid exposure without asserting diagnosis or cause.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with rosacea 2021

sources_used.title: British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of people with rosacea 2021
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation

additions.text: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.
additions.rationale: The exact differential section supports documenting a perioral eruption context, onset and persistence, papular or pustular morphology, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning or soreness, distribution, and relevant corticosteroid exposure without asserting diagnosis or cause.

source_limitations: Exact age, sites, dimensions, exposure timing, cosmetics and contact factors, pregnancy, diagnosis, differential, investigations, medicine, treatment, referral, safety net, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
